sources.name: Overleaf: Online LaTeX Editor with Real-time Collaboration
sources.description: World's most popular online LaTeX editor with 20M+ users. Features real-time collaboration, 1000+ templates, visual editor, track changes, Git sync, and TeX Live 2025 support. Acquired by Digital Science. AI Assist add-on (via Writefull) launched 2025. Key limitation: no offline support, compilation queues under heavy load.

sources.name: Overleaf Open Source Repository
sources.description: Open-source codebase for Overleaf. Self-hostable Community Edition under AGPL license. Server Pro available for institutional deployment. Node.js backend with Docker-based compilation.

sources.name: Digital Science AI Writing Tools for Overleaf Users
sources.description: Digital Science launched AI Assist for Overleaf in 2025 powered by Writefull. Features: context-aware language feedback, LaTeX error assistance, LaTeX code generation from prompts/images (TeXGPT), and figure generation. Available as freemium add-on.

sources.name: Typst: The New Foundation for Documents
sources.description: Modern typesetting system designed as a LaTeX alternative. Written in Rust with dramatically faster compilation. Markdown-inspired syntax, native Unicode support, incremental compilation, and real-time preview. Web app with collaborative editing, teams, and GitHub/GitLab sync. Open-source compiler, commercial web app.

sources.name: Typst GitHub Repository
sources.description: Open-source Typst compiler on GitHub. Written in Rust, Apache 2.0 licensed. Features incremental compilation, scripting language with closures, pattern matching, and package management via Typst Universe.

sources.name: Google NotebookLM: AI-Powered Research Assistant
sources.description: Google's AI research assistant powered by Gemini. Key features: source-grounded responses to prevent hallucination, Deep Research (autonomous web research synthesizing 15-25 sources in 3-5 min), Audio Overview (podcast generation from sources), and expanded file support (Sheets, Drive, images, PDFs, docx). Clover adopts similar source management philosophy.

sources.name: NotebookLM Adds Deep Research and Support for More Source Types
sources.description: Official Google blog post announcing Deep Research for NotebookLM (November 2025). Two modes: Fast Research (30-45 sec, 10-15 sources) and Deep Research (3-5 min, 15-25 sources). Results and sources added directly to notebook. Background operation while user continues work.

sources.name: Prism: A Free LaTeX-Native Workspace for Scientists by OpenAI
sources.description: OpenAI's free LaTeX-native workspace for scientists launched January 2026. Integrates GPT-5.2 for idea exploration, hypothesis testing, and step-by-step reasoning. Combines document editing, compilation, citation management, and AI-assisted revision. Free for all ChatGPT account holders.

sources.name: Introducing Prism - OpenAI Blog
sources.description: Official OpenAI blog post introducing Prism. Designed to accelerate human scientists' work, analogous to how Cursor/Windsurf work in software development. Drafting, revision, collaboration, and publication preparation in a single cloud-based LaTeX-native workspace.

sources.name: OpenAI Launches Prism, a New AI Workspace for Scientists - TechCrunch
sources.description: TechCrunch coverage of OpenAI Prism launch. Analysis of Prism's position in the scientific writing tools market and comparison with existing solutions.

sources.name: Elicit: AI for Scientific Research
sources.description: AI research assistant searching 138M+ academic papers and 545K clinical trials. Features: Systematic Review workflow with Strict Screening, Research Agents for complex workflows (Dec 2025), up to 20,000 data points per analysis, 1,000 papers per run. Integrated Claude Opus 4.5 (Dec 2025). Sentence-level citations for transparency.

sources.name: SciSpace: AI Research Agent with 150+ Tools
sources.description: AI research agent integrating 150+ tools and databases (PubMed, Google Scholar, arXiv, grants.gov) with 280M+ papers. Features: Deep Review (automated mini literature reviews), AI Writer with auto-citations, systematic literature review with audit-ready workflows, manuscript-to-slides conversion, and AI content detection.

sources.name: The AI Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery
sources.citation: @article{Lu2024AIScientist, author = {Lu, Chris and Lu, Cong and Lange, Robert Tjarko and Foerster, Jakob and Clune, Jeff and Ha, David}, title = {The {AI} Scientist: Towards Fully Automated Open-Ended Scientific Discovery}, journal = {arXiv preprint arXiv:2408.06292}, year = {2024}}
sources.description: Sakana AI's system for fully automated scientific research. Generates novel ideas, implements code, runs experiments, writes full papers, and conducts simulated peer review. Papers produced at <$15 each, some exceeding ML conference acceptance thresholds. Authors: Chris Lu, Cong Lu, Robert Tjarko Lange, Jakob Foerster, Jeff Clune, David Ha.

sources.name: Agent Laboratory: Using LLM Agents as Research Assistants
sources.citation: @article{Schmidgall2025AgentLab, author = {Schmidgall, Samuel and Su, Yusheng and Wang, Ze and Sun, Ximeng and Wu, Jialian and Yu, Xiaodong and Liu, Jiang and Moor, Michael and Liu, Zicheng and Barsoum, Emad}, title = {Agent Laboratory: Using {LLM} Agents as Research Assistants}, journal = {arXiv preprint arXiv:2501.04227}, year = {2025}}
sources.description: Autonomous LLM-based research framework with three phases: Literature Review, Experimentation, and Report Writing. Specialized agents collaborate using arXiv, Hugging Face, Python, and LaTeX. Human feedback at each stage. o1-preview achieves best outcomes. Authors: Schmidgall et al. (AMD, Johns Hopkins, ETH Zurich).

sources.name: Claude Code: AI Coding Agent by Anthropic
sources.description: Anthropic's AI coding agent operating in terminal, VS Code, JetBrains, and web. Features: MCP (Model Context Protocol) for external tool integration, CLAUDE.md persistent instructions, custom commands, hooks, multi-agent support with sub-agents, and CI/CD integration. Core technology powering Clover's AI capabilities.

sources.name: Claude Code Documentation
sources.description: Official Claude Code documentation. Covers setup, CLAUDE.md configuration, custom commands, hooks, MCP integration, Plan Mode, sub-agents, and GitHub Actions integration. Essential reference for understanding Clover's underlying AI infrastructure.

sources.name: Local-First Software: You Own Your Data, in spite of the Cloud
sources.citation: @inproceedings{Kleppmann2019LocalFirst, author = {Kleppmann, Martin and Wiggins, Adam and van Hardenberg, Peter and McGranaghan, Mark}, title = {Local-first software: you own your data, in spite of the cloud}, booktitle = {Proceedings of the 2019 ACM SIGPLAN International Symposium on New Ideas, New Paradigms, and Reflections on Programming and Software (Onward!)}, pages = {154--178}, year = {2019}, doi = {10.1145/3359591.3359737}}
sources.description: Foundational paper proposing local-first software principles: data ownership, offline capability, collaboration without cloud dependency, and long-term preservation. Surveys approaches from email to web apps, proposes CRDTs as foundational technology. By Kleppmann, Wiggins, van Hardenberg, McGranaghan (Ink & Switch). Directly influences Clover's local-first design philosophy.

sources.name: Local-First Software: Seven Ideals - Ink & Switch Essay
sources.description: Full PDF of the local-first software paper. Defines seven ideals: (1) no spinners (fast), (2) your work is not trapped on one device, (3) the network is optional, (4) seamless collaboration, (5) the long now (longevity), (6) security and privacy by default, (7) you retain ultimate ownership and control. Directly relevant to Clover's architecture decisions.

sources.name: Zotero: Your Personal Research Assistant
sources.description: Open-source reference manager by a nonprofit organization. Features: web browser integration for one-click import, 9,000+ citation styles, integrated PDF/ePUB/HTML reader with annotations, Word/LibreOffice/Google Docs plugins, cross-device sync, group libraries for collaboration. Zotero 7 (Aug 2024): dark mode, redesigned UI, Android app (Jun 2025). No financial interest in user data.

sources.name: Mendeley: Free Reference Manager by Elsevier
sources.description: Elsevier's free cloud-based reference manager. Features: PDF highlighting and annotation, Mendeley Cite add-in for Word (standalone), Web Importer browser extension, smart collections, cloud-synced library across devices, Mendeley Notebook for notes, and integration with Scopus/ScienceDirect. Proprietary platform tied to Elsevier ecosystem.

sources.name: Clover: AI-Powered Academic Writing Tool (GitHub Repository)
sources.description: Official GitHub repository for Clover, an AI-powered academic writing tool. Combines a LaTeX editor, PDF preview, Claude Code terminal, and NotebookLM-style source management in a single local-first desktop application. Built with Electron, React, Monaco Editor, and xterm.
